mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount factors, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden unemployment rate spike above 5.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03 during expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month drop in industrial production below negative 0.5 percent reflects immediate supply-side bottlenecks, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of periods following an INDPRO decline below -0.5%, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance during supply shocks.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation exceeds 3.5 percent while the federal funds rate remains below 3.0 percent, real rates turn deeply negative, prompting equity market repricing and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of periods meeting the trigger, or the real rate differential shows no statistical relationship with equity returns with Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls above 200000 per month signals robust economic activity and corporate earnings expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods when PAYEMS exceeds 200000, or the payroll growth signal yields a Rank IC below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP coinciding with falling retail sales confirms a recessionary demand shock, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDP and retail sales decline fails to predict negative 20-day returns in >50% of historical recession episodes, or the joint signal out-of-sample accuracy is indistinguishable from random.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stable inflation indicates healthy capacity utilization and margin expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods when INDPRO rises and CPIAUCSL is stable, or the signal predictive power vanishes with Rank IC below 0.02 across market cycles.
